---
title: "Question 1 - Chi-square test (Gender vs Ice-cream flavor)"
author: "Upayan Mazumder"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
alpha <- 0.05
```

## Question

From the given contingency table, test whether gender and ice-cream flavor preference are associated.

```{r data}
# Frequencies read from the question image
tbl <- matrix(
  c(100, 120, 60,
    350, 320, 150),
  nrow = 2,
  byrow = TRUE,
  dimnames = list(
    Gender = c("Men", "Women"),
    Flavor = c("Chocolate", "Vanilla", "Strawberry")
  )
)

tbl
sum(tbl)  # Table total (image text mentions 920, but tabulated counts sum to 1100)
```

## Hypotheses

- H0: Gender and flavor preference are independent.
- H1: Gender and flavor preference are associated.

## Chi-square test of independence

```{r chi-square-test}
chi_result <- chisq.test(tbl, correct = FALSE)
chi_result
chi_result$expected
min(chi_result$expected)
```

```{r decision}
if (chi_result$p.value < alpha) {
  cat("Decision: Reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: There is a significant association between gender and flavor preference.")
} else {
  cat("Decision: Fail to reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: No significant association is detected between gender and flavor preference.")
}
```
